scope_boundary: Public-safe local external Circle strided candidate-fanout receipt import only. The slice records one pinned `CC-AI-CONTRACT-FANOUT-001` structural fixture at Circle commit `63b0f511`, `context_length=12`, `stride=5`, `gcd=1`, `predicted_reach=12`, `full_coverage=true`, `candidate_budget=12`, `duplicate_count=0`, four required theorem IDs, two planner recommendations, contract fingerprint `d4c878563747da9c9f1f55cd689f04e2a0a8e31ce9429a138341ec4e27ee3799`, command-output digests, and passing targeted Circle CLI test summaries. It does not prove search quality, retrieval quality, routing quality, sparse-attention quality, long-context quality, model quality, speed, memory savings, transfer, safety, ASI, or Coil chapter-core promotion.
transition_reason: The strided fanout receipt slice makes one Circle finite stride-coverage and duplicate-budget accounting fixture concrete in the book and closes the stale 'no fanout receipt import' blocker for the Coil chapter. The evidence remains an external-project receipt import with no deployed ASI-side fanout trace, no search, retrieval, or routing workload, no runtime or memory measurement, no model-quality result, no optimal-schedule result, and no independent review, so it blocks promotion rather than moving support state.
required_artifacts: fresh public Circle replay or archived public strided-fanout contract pack
repository-owned command that reruns the relevant Circle fanout proof target or verifies an archived replay bundle
deployed or replayed ASI-side candidate-fanout trace
ordinary sequential, random, round-robin, local-window, dense-attention, retrieval, and learned-routing baselines with workload metrics
search-quality, retrieval-quality, routing-quality, long-context, runtime, memory, and model-quality measurements for any promoted claim
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
